story.iWant: Widget aggregate enhanced with tenant context validation
story.soThat: the demo application demonstrates multi-tenancy correctly
story.tasks: - AC1: Widget.kt commands include tenantId field - AC2: CreateWidgetCommand includes tenant_id from TenantContext - AC3: Command handler validates tenant context (Layer 2) - AC4: Widget events include tenant_id in metadata - AC5: widget_view projection table includes tenant_id column - AC6: Integration test creates widgets for multiple tenants - AC7: Cross-tenant access test validates isolation (tenant A cannot see tenant B widgets) - AC8: All Widget tests pass with tenant context

story-context.acceptanceCriteria: 1. Widget.kt commands include tenantId field 2. CreateWidgetCommand includes tenant_id from TenantContext 3. Command handler validates tenant context (Layer 2) 4. Widget events include tenant_id in metadata 5. widget_view projection table includes tenant_id column 6. Integration test creates widgets for multiple tenants 7. Cross-tenant access test validates isolation (tenant A cannot see tenant B widgets) 8. All Widget tests pass with tenant context

doc.relevance: Widget aggregate serves as reference implementation for multi-tenancy patterns
doc.snippet: Widget Demo as Multi-Tenancy Reference Implementation: - Demonstrates all 3 layers of tenant isolation - Commands implement TenantAwareCommand interface with tenantId field - Command handlers validate tenant context (Layer 2) - Events enriched with tenant_id metadata for async processors (Story 4.5) - Projection schema includes tenant_id column with RLS policies (Story 4.4) - Integration tests validate cross-tenant isolation

doc.relevance: Widget aggregate patterns from Epic 2 to be enhanced with multi-tenancy
doc.snippet: Epic 2 Widget Implementation: - Widget aggregate with CreateWidgetCommand, PublishWidgetCommand - WidgetCreatedEvent, WidgetPublishedEvent for event sourcing - WidgetProjectionEventHandler for read model updates - widget_view projection table with jOOQ repository - Integration tests with Testcontainers PostgreSQL

doc.relevance: Business requirement for multi-tenant Widget demo
doc.snippet: Widget demo must demonstrate production-ready multi-tenancy. All 3 layers of tenant isolation validated in demo application. Cross-tenant isolation tests prove security guarantees.

Story 4.6: Multi-Tenant Widget Demo Enhancement - Widget.kt commands include tenantId field - CreateWidgetCommand includes tenant_id from TenantContext - Command handler validates tenant context (Layer 2) - Widget events include tenant_id in metadata - widget_view projection table includes tenant_id column

artifact.reason: Widget aggregate to be enhanced with tenantId field and validation
Widget commands to implement TenantAwareCommand interface
Widget events to include tenant_id in metadata
WidgetView projection to include tenant_id column
Flyway migration to add tenant_id column to widget_view table
Existing Widget integration tests to be enhanced with multi-tenant scenarios

constraints.constraint: Enhance existing Widget aggregate WITHOUT breaking Epic 2 functionality. Add tenantId field to commands, maintain existing behavior. All Epic 2 Widget tests must still pass after enhancement.

All Widget commands implement TenantAwareCommand interface (Story 4.3). Commands: CreateWidgetCommand, PublishWidgetCommand, ArchiveWidgetCommand. Each command includes override val tenantId: String field.
Widget command handlers validate tenant context (Layer 2). Pattern: require(command.tenantId == TenantContext.getCurrentTenantId()) Generic error message: "Access denied: tenant context mismatch"
Widget events enriched with tenant_id metadata (Story 4.5 pattern). Use apply(event, MetaData.with("tenant_id", tenantId)) in aggregate. Metadata propagates to async event handlers.

Integration tests validate: tenant A cannot see tenant B widgets. Test scenarios: create widget as tenant-a, query as tenant-b → no results. Test all layers: API (Layer 1), Command (Layer 2), Database (Layer 3).

interface.name: Widget TenantAware Commands
interface.description: Widget commands implement TenantAwareCommand interface with tenantId field. Commands extract tenant_id from TenantContext at API layer.
interface.pattern:
data class CreateWidgetCommand( val widgetId: UUID, val name: String, override val tenantId: String ) : TenantAwareCommand
// API layer creates command with tenant context: val tenantId = TenantContext().getCurrentTenantId() val command = CreateWidgetCommand( widgetId = UUID.randomUUID(), name = request.name, tenantId = tenantId )

interface.name: Widget Command Handler Validation
interface.description: Widget aggregate validates tenant context in command handler (Layer 2). Validation happens BEFORE event application.

interface.name: Widget Projection with Tenant Column
interface.description: WidgetView projection includes tenant_id column for RLS enforcement. Event handler extracts tenant from context for projection updates.

standards.standard: Constitutional TDD: Integration tests BEFORE Widget enhancement. Test validates multi-tenant scenarios with all 3 layers.

Test scenarios for comprehensive multi-tenancy validation: - Tenant A creates widget → Tenant A can query widget - Tenant B creates widget → Tenant B can query widget - Tenant A queries → cannot see Tenant B widgets (isolation) - Tenant A commands → cannot modify Tenant B aggregates (Layer 2) - Direct SQL query as Tenant A → RLS blocks Tenant B data (Layer 3)

All Epic 2 Widget tests must pass after enhancement. Verify no regression in existing Widget functionality. Tests: WidgetAggregateTest, WidgetProjectionEventHandlerIntegrationTest, WidgetControllerIntegrationTest.
Use Keycloak Testcontainers with multiple users for multi-tenant tests. Generate JWTs for tenant-a and tenant-b with different tenant_id claims. Follow Epic 3 KeycloakIntegrationTest pattern.

ideas.idea: Test AC1: Verify Widget commands include tenantId field (TenantAwareCommand)
Test AC2: CreateWidgetCommand includes tenant_id from TenantContext
Test AC3: Command handler validates tenant context matches command.tenantId
Test AC4: Widget events enriched with tenant_id metadata
Test AC5: widget_view projection table includes tenant_id column (schema validation)
Test AC6: Create widgets for tenant-a and tenant-b (multi-tenant scenario)
Test AC7: Tenant A queries widgets → only sees tenant-a widgets (cross-tenant isolation)
Test AC8: All Epic 2 Widget tests still pass (backward compatibility)
Test Layer 2 validation: Tenant A command with tenant-b tenantId → TenantIsolationException
Test Layer 3 RLS: Direct SQL query as tenant-a → cannot see tenant-b widgets
Test concurrent multi-tenant widget creation → isolated contexts
Test projection event handler receives tenant context from metadata (Story 4.5)
